description: You are a radiation effects engineer for a space agency. Configure GPredict to analyze long-term satellite passes over the South Atlantic Anomaly (SAA). Create a ground station named 'Tristan_da_Cunha' at 37.1052 S, 12.2777 W with an altitude of 20 meters. Create a tracking module named 'SAA_Targets' containing four critical Earth Observation satellites: METOP-B (38771), METOP-C (43689), SUOMI NPP (37849), and NOAA 19 (33591). Ensure the Tristan_da_Cunha ground station is assigned to the SAA_Targets module. Adjust the global preferences to predict 50 passes and enable both the map grid and the terminator line. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'. GPredict is already open.

natural_language.prompt: I need to set up GPredict to forecast upcoming passes over the South Atlantic Anomaly for radiation analysis. Please create a ground station called Tristan_da_Cunha located at 37.1052 South, 12.2777 West, altitude 20 meters. Next, create a tracking module named SAA_Targets and add these four satellites: METOP-B (38771), METOP-C (43689), SUOMI NPP (37849), and NOAA 19 (33591). Make sure you bind the SAA_Targets module to use the Tristan_da_Cunha ground station you just made. Finally, go into the global preferences and do three things: change the number of predicted passes to 50, enable the map grid, and enable the map terminator.
